title: ATH Protocol Schema v0.1
description: JSON Schema definitions for the Agent Trust Handshake Protocol v0.1. These types define the request and response objects for all ATH API endpoints.

DiscoveryDocument.description: Gateway discovery document returned by GET /.well-known/ath.json. Lists available providers and the agent registration endpoint.

ath_version.description: Protocol version implemented by this gateway.

agent_registration_endpoint.description: URL where agents submit registration requests.

supported_providers.description: List of service providers available through this gateway.

ServiceDiscoveryDocument.description: Service-side discovery document returned by GET /.well-known/ath-app.json. Published by services that natively support ATH (Native Mode).

ath_version.description: Protocol version supported by this service.

api_base.description: Base URL for the service's API.

ServiceAuthConfig.description: Authentication configuration published by an ATH-native service.

scopes_supported.description: OAuth scopes supported by this service.

agent_attestation_required.description: Whether the service requires agent attestation JWTs.

ProviderInfo.description: Information about a service provider available through the gateway.

provider_id.description: Unique identifier for this provider within the gateway.

available_scopes.description: Scopes that agents can request for this provider.

auth_mode.description: Authentication mode used by this provider.

agent_approval_required.description: Whether agents must be registered and approved before accessing this provider.

AgentIdentityDocument.description: Agent identity document published at the agent's Agent_ID URI (e.g. https://agent.example.com/.well-known/agent.json). Contains the agent's metadata and public key for attestation verification.

public_key.description: JWK or PEM public key for agent attestation verification.

DeveloperInfo.description: Information about the agent's developer or organization.

AgentRegistrationRequest.description: Request body for POST /ath/agents/register. Registers a new agent with the ATH implementor (Phase A: app-side authorization).

agent_attestation.description: Signed JWT (ES256) proving agent identity. Must include exp, aud, iss, sub claims.

requested_providers.description: Providers and scopes the agent is requesting access to.

purpose.description: Human-readable description of the agent's purpose.

redirect_uris.description: OAuth callback URIs for this agent.

ProviderScopeRequest.description: A request for access to a specific provider with specific scopes.

scopes.description: Scopes being requested for this provider.

AgentRegistrationResponse.description: Response from POST /ath/agents/register. Contains the agent's credentials and per-provider approval results.

client_id.description: Unique identifier for this agent registration.

client_secret.description: Secret for authenticating token exchange requests. Store securely.

approval_expires.description: When this registration expires. ISO 8601 timestamp.

ProviderApproval.description: The result of an agent's registration request for a specific provider. The implementor MAY approve a subset of requested scopes.

approved_scopes.description: Scopes that were approved for the agent.

denied_scopes.description: Scopes that were denied for the agent.

AuthorizationRequest.description: Request body for POST /ath/authorize. Initiates the user-side OAuth authorization flow (Phase B). The implementor generates PKCE parameters (RFC 7636) server-side.

agent_attestation.description: Fresh signed JWT proving agent identity.

provider_id.description: Which provider to authorize access to.

scopes.description: Scopes to request. Must be within the agent's approved scopes for this provider.

user_redirect_uri.description: Where to redirect the user after OAuth consent.

state.description: Opaque state parameter for CSRF protection.

resource.description: Target resource server URI. Optional, per RFC 8707 (Resource Indicators).

AuthorizationResponse.description: Response from POST /ath/authorize. Contains the OAuth consent URL (with PKCE parameters) and a session identifier.

authorization_url.description: URL where the user should be directed for OAuth consent. Includes code_challenge and code_challenge_method=S256 (PKCE).

ath_session_id.description: Session identifier used during token exchange.

TokenExchangeRequest.description: Request body for POST /ath/token. Exchanges an OAuth authorization code for an ATH access token after user consent.

code.description: OAuth authorization code from the callback.

ath_session_id.description: Session ID from the authorization step.

TokenResponse.description: Response from POST /ath/token. Contains the ATH access token with scope intersection breakdown showing how effective permissions were derived.

access_token.description: The ATH access token. Bound to (agent_id, user_id, provider_id, scopes).

effective_scopes.description: The computed effective scopes (result of scope intersection).

provider_id.description: Which provider this token grants access to.

agent_id.description: The agent this token is bound to.

ScopeIntersection.description: Breakdown of the scope intersection computation. Effective = Agent Approved ∩ User Consented ∩ Requested.

agent_approved.description: Scopes the service approved for this agent (Phase A).

user_consented.description: Scopes the user consented to in the OAuth flow (Phase B).

effective.description: The intersection — the actual permissions the token carries.

TokenRevocationRequest.description: Request body for POST /ath/revoke. Immediately invalidates an ATH access token.

token.description: The ATH access token to revoke.

ATHError.description: Standard error response returned by all ATH endpoints on failure.
